briefing.incidentSummary:
A fresh graduate paid ₹25,000 as a 'security deposit' for a data entry job at a company called DataPro Solutions.

The company doesn't exist. The phone number is dead. The money is gone.

The job posting was on a fake careers website that mimicked a real firm. Someone ran this scam across at least 14 victims in one month.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Trace the fake job posting to its creator

› Identify the bank account used to collect deposits

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Ramesh P
suspects.profileNotes: Registered the fake careers website using his personal email. The UPI collection account is linked to his Aadhaar. Has a prior fraud case from 2022 involving a similar job scam.

suspects.name: Sunita M
suspects.profileNotes: Victim of brand impersonation — her company's name was cloned. Filed a complaint immediately. Not involved in the fraud.

suspects.name: Vikash T
suspects.profileNotes: Approved the job listing without verifying the company. Negligent but not complicit. The fake site bypassed the portal's domain check.

items.label: Congratulations! You are selected for Data Entry Executive. Pay ₹25,000 security deposit to confirm your slot.
items.detail: Message sent from +91-98XXXXXXXX — a prepaid SIM registered to a false identity. Sent to 14 different victims using the same script. The security deposit demand is the fraud trigger.

items.label: I paid the deposit. When do I get the offer letter?
items.detail: Standard victim response. After payment, the fake HR number went silent. Victim filed a complaint 3 days later when offer letter never arrived.

items.label: Payment received. Your offer letter will be sent in 48 hours.
items.detail: Sent immediately after the UPI transfer completed — automated confirmation script. The 48-hour delay was designed to give the scammer time to withdraw cash before the victim raised an alarm.

items.label: fake_site_registration.txt
items.detail: Domain datapro-careers.in registered 6 weeks before the scam. Registrant email: rameshpune44@gmail.com. IP used for registration: 49.36.44.201 — geolocates to Pune, Kothrud.

items.label: upi_account_trace.pdf
items.detail: UPI ID datapro.hr@upi traced to a savings account opened under Ramesh P's Aadhaar number. ₹3.5 lakh received across 14 transactions in one month — all immediately withdrawn as cash.

hiddenItem.label: prior_fraud_fir.pdf
hiddenItem.detail: FIR from 2022 against Ramesh P for an identical job scam in Nashik — same MO: fake company name, prepaid SIM, UPI deposit collection. Case settled with fine but no jail time. Pattern confirmed.

minigame.title: Decode Registrant Email Username
minigame.hint: Caesar shift -3. What is the first name in the registration email?
hints: It is a common Indian first name.

onSuccess.message: prior_fraud_fir.pdf is now available in your file feed.

items.label: Victim Pattern Analysis
items.detail: All 14 victims responded to the same job listing on QuickHire portal. All paid between ₹20,000–₹30,000. All contacted within a 30-day window. Suggests one automated campaign with a single operator.

items.label: Website Clone Analysis
items.detail: datapro-careers.in used a cloned template of the real DataPro Solutions site with the logo, colour scheme, and address copied. Only the contact number and bank details were changed. Built using a free website builder — no coding skill needed.

items.label: Domain Registration IP
items.detail: IP 49.36.44.201 used to register both the fake domain and the Wix account that hosts the cloned site. Geolocates to a residential address in Kothrud, Pune — matching Ramesh P's known address.

timeline.title: Fake Site Goes Live
timeline.description: Cloned DataPro website published on Wix

timeline.title: Job Listing Posted
timeline.description: Fake Data Entry role posted on QuickHire portal

timeline.title: 14 Victims Targeted
timeline.description: ₹3.5 lakh collected via UPI across 14 payments

timeline.title: Victim Files Complaint
timeline.description: Graduate reports scam to cyber cell after no offer letter

timeline.title: Domain Trace Begins
timeline.description: WHOIS lookup links domain to rameshpune44@gmail.com

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Ramesh P exposed as the job scam operator.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The real culprit was Ramesh P.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

aria.welcomeToHub:
...A dream job that never existed.
14 people paid. None got hired.
Find who built the lie.
aria.exploreFeeds:
Domain records. UPI traces. Cloned websites.
This scam was lazy — they left their real email in the WHOIS.
aria.viewEvidence:
Same script. Same UPI. Same 48-hour delay.
One person ran all of this.
aria.decryptionHint:
The registration email has a name in it.
Decode the first 6 characters.
aria.flagSuspect:
A trace is forming.
You're on to something — keep pulling the thread.
More fragments remain. Don't stop now.
